Hi Victor. You mentioned a problem with the security hardware order. What is going on?
Thanks for making time, Priya. To be concise: the shipment is three weeks late, and there is a discrepancy between what we ordered and what the supplier confirmed.
I have sent two emails with no reply. I would appreciate it if you could liaise with the supplier from the London side, since they are your recipient of record.
Of course. Can you send me only the pertinent details? The order number, the dates, and the figures that do not match.
I will send a concise summary right after this call. As a courtesy, I will copy you on the follow-up so the supplier sees we are aligned.
Good. And if they still go quiet? I do not want this to drag on with two outstanding items.
Then we stay tactful but firm. If we do not receive a reply by Friday, we will escalate the matter to the regional director and reiterate the original requirements.
Perfect. Send me the summary and I will reply with a prompt update. Thank you, Victor.
I will circulate the follow-up email to all regional leads as a courtesy.
Once we reach a consensus, I will confirm the decision in writing.
I would appreciate it if you could send me the report by Thursday.
I am writing to follow up on my previous email about the outstanding order.
If we do not receive a reply by Friday, we will have to escalate the matter.
I was wondering if you could clarify the discrepancy in the figures.
Thank you for your prompt reply; I look forward to hearing from you.

Hi Victor, this is Daniel Okoro from Global Procurement. I am calling to follow up on the security hardware order for the LATAM region. I sent two emails last week, but I have not received a response, so I wanted to flag this as a matter of urgency. To be concise: the shipment is now three weeks overdue, and there is a discrepancy between the quantity we ordered and the quantity confirmed by the supplier. I would appreciate it if your team could look into this today. If we do not resolve the outstanding items by Friday, I will have to escalate the matter to the regional vice president, which none of us want. I have included the relevant figures in a follow-up email for your reference. Please liaise with the supplier and reiterate our original requirements. I would be grateful for a prompt reply at your earliest convenience. Thank you, Victor. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Good afternoon, team. This is a short announcement from the Communications Office regarding our new correspondence guidelines, effective Monday. As a matter of courtesy, every external email should now open with a clear subject line and a brief, polite greeting. We are asking everyone to keep messages concise and to include only pertinent information, so that recipients can act quickly. When you make a request, please be tactful. Instead of writing send this immediately, write I would appreciate it if you could send this by the end of the day. If an issue remains unresolved after two follow-ups, you are encouraged to escalate it through the proper channel rather than copying the entire leadership team. Finally, please remember to liaise with the relevant department before promising a deadline on their behalf. We believe these small changes in register will bring more clarity and goodwill to our daily communication. Thank you for your cooperation, and please reach out to the Communications Office if you have any questions.
